questions.question: A group of students are asked to rate the difficulty of three math problems on a scale of 1 to 5. The results are as follows: 5, 5, 3, 1, 5, 4, 5, 3, 1. Is the rating distribution symmetric?
options: D) The data is too small
questions.explanation: Chi-square test is used to check if observed frequencies are significantly different from expected frequencies, but it cannot be used to check for symmetry.

questions.question: In a quality control process, a manufacturer produces 20 light bulbs and 40 defective light bulbs. Is this a good fit for a Poisson distribution?
options: C) It depends on the parameter of the Poisson distribution
questions.explanation: The observed frequencies (20, 40) do not resemble the expected Poisson distribution, which would have frequencies of 20e^(-1), 40e^(-2), etc.

questions.question: A researcher conducts a survey on the favorite colors of students at a university. The results are: 40 blue, 20 green, 10 red, 10 blue, 20 green, 5 red. Is this a good fit for a binomial distribution?
options: C) It depends on the parameter of the binomial distribution
questions.explanation: The observed frequencies (40, 20, 10, 10) resemble the expected binomial distribution, with probability of success being 0.6.

questions.question: A company sells two products A and B. The sales figures are: 1000, 800, 1200, 900, 1500, 1000. Is this a good fit for a normal distribution?
options: C) It depends on the parameter of the normal distribution
questions.explanation: The observed frequencies (1000, 800, 1200, 900, 1500, 1000) do not resemble the expected normal distribution, which would have a bell-shaped curve.

questions.question: A researcher conducts a survey on the heights of students at a university. The results are: 160, 165, 168, 155, 170, 162, 165, 168, 160. Is this a good fit for a Poisson distribution?
options: C) It depends on the parameter of the Poisson distribution
questions.explanation: The observed frequencies (160, 165, 168, 155, 170, 162, 165, 168, 160) do not resemble the expected Poisson distribution, which would have frequencies of 160e^(-1), 165e^(-2), etc.
